features.summary: Broadcom reported Q1 FY2021 revenue of $6.7 billion, up 14% year-over-year, with Semiconductor Solutions at $4.9 billion (+17% YoY) and Infrastructure Software at $1.7 billion (+5% YoY). The company has implemented rigorous backlog management processes to address supply-demand imbalances, extending lead times across products while maintaining discipline in matching supply to real end-user consumption. Wireless revenue hit seasonal peak at 40% of semiconductor mix, driven by higher FBAR content and Wi-Fi 6/6E shipments, while networking showed strong 15% YoY growth with bookings up 80% YoY.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 revenue of $6.5 billion with 13% YoY growth, maintaining similar growth trajectory through 2021. Wireless is expected to show typical seasonal decline in Q2 but sustain 30-40% YoY growth through the second half. Networking demand remains robust driven by cloud and telco infrastructure upgrades, while broadband should see double-digit YoY growth in Q2. The company is 90% booked for 2021 with extended lead times, and management believes the current high growth rates reflect accelerated connectivity platform adoption during the pandemic.
features.risks: The primary risk is potential demand normalization as supply-demand imbalances resolve and lead times return to normal levels. Server storage connectivity remains weak due to enterprise softness and inventory depletion at OEMs. There's uncertainty about when supply will catch up to demand sufficiently that customers no longer feel obligated to build inventory cushions. The sustainability of current elevated growth rates beyond 2021 is questionable as they may represent pulled-forward demand rather than structural increases.
features.key_drivers: Strong secular demand for connectivity solutions driven by work-from-home trends, cloud infrastructure scaling, and 5G/Wi-Fi 6 adoption. The company's technology leadership position in critical connectivity markets provides pricing power and customer stickiness. Infrastructure software benefits from mission-critical enterprise applications that see increased usage during the pandemic. Disciplined supply chain management and non-cancelable booking terms help maintain pricing discipline while ensuring real demand visibility across the customer base.
